item.title: Okay, but derivative
item.content:encoded:
I just couldn't like this SCP. The 'emaciated humanoid', the autopsies with nonsensical injuries, the camera feed cutting out immediately before the monster tosses the MTF's salad…It really just felt like an underwater SCP-087, except 087 at least gave the reader a sense of tension by providing the opportunity for the D-class to escape from danger.

(Also…morse code? I get that encoding messages is a trope as old as time, but I could not keep a straight face imagining 4523-01 (who I imagined as 096 with flippers on) clicking his tongue for the viewing and listening pleasure of the Foundation.)

Granted, it PASSED as an SCP! The page layout was tit-for-tat, it mixed thalassophobia with dysmorphophobia well enough to embed itself into the nightmares of any 15 year old, and honestly, I was a little unsettled by it going through my first organic read-through. If it was 2008 and this was competing for a Series I spot, it definitely would have made it, of this I have no doubt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think cutting the feed was the opposite of coldness.
There was an unavoidable death coming, and assuming more than one person back at base is listening to this, they're sparing their coworkers the trauma of hearing that death. I'm sure it kept recording, just not broadcasting in that room. That way only one person has to hear it to assess the recording for further relevant information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was pulled into the article at first, but was a bit dissapointed when it turned out a monster was explicitly demonically-torturing the divers. Liked it when it was unclear (to the foundation atleast) that there was a monster in there, felt scarier
Just my taste though, I still like it and +1 for sure
Little things:
"Some researchers have claimed that an entity resembling a humanoid can be observed on the left side of the frame. In the final frames of the recording, it is speculated that a "smile" can be seen before the feed cuts to black." Either they can see a figure or they can't, hard for me to imagine the "claim" and "speculated" bits
I liked the coldness of FCC like "prepare to cut the feed" wow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Satisfyingly obscure and unsettling, not too deeply explained.
This is perfect, and yet, I'd somewhat want to read more on it.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added some tags. Feel free to add more.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have not sourced the image used in this article, and as such may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0). You have 48 hours to source the image used. If you have not sourced the image within the allotted time frame, or the source is noncompliant with the SCP wiki's license, then we'll have to take the image down.

EDIT: While the image has been sourced, it is not cc-compliant, as the source holds all rights and copyright to images used. As such, the image has been removed.
